key_insight: HiReLC uses a two-level reinforcement learning controller to jointly prune and quantize neural networks while allocating compression decisions using sensitivity-aware global coordination.
engineering_relevance: The method offers an architecture-agnostic way to compress vision models with strong storage savings, making it useful for deploying larger networks under memory and compute constraints.
method: Low-level agents choose per-block compression settings over bitwidth, pruning ratio, quantization type, and granularity.
High-level agents coordinate global budget allocation through ensemble voting informed by Fisher Information sensitivity.
An active-learning loop uses a lightweight surrogate and post-compression fine-tuning to reduce evaluation cost.
evidence_signal: Across Vision Transformer and CNN benchmarks, the approach achieved about 5.99–6.72× parameter-storage compression with accuracy changes ranging from a 3.83% gain in one case to 0.55–5.62% drops in others.
watch_item: The surrogate is only a reward-shaping tool, so the method still depends on expensive final evaluation and fine-tuning, which may limit scalability.
paper_title: Hierarchical Reinforcement Learning for Neural Network Compression (HiReLC): Pruning and Quantization
